pattern: For every window, precompute (via a Z-function both forwards and on the reversed strings) the longest exact prefix match and the longest exact suffix match against the pattern -- a window is almost equal exactly when those two matched lengths, plus one allowed change in between, cover the whole pattern.
hints: Precompute, for every starting index i, the length of the longest prefix of pattern that matches s starting at i exactly (a Z-function over pattern + separator + s does this in linear time).
Do the same thing on the reversed strings to get, for every ending index, the longest suffix of pattern that matches s ending there exactly.

A window starting at i is almost equal to pattern if the front-match length equals the whole pattern (zero changes needed), or if front-match-length + back-match-length covers all but at most one character of pattern (one change fixes the gap).

edge_inputs.description: pattern occurs exactly, zero changes needed
pattern is a single character, matches immediately
the mismatch has to land right at the start of the window
the mismatch has to land right at the end of the window
no window comes within one change of pattern
pattern length is one less than s's length
several windows are almost equal, must report the smallest index

stress.description: random s/pattern over a tiny alphabet to make near-matches common at scale
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 4000))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('ab') for _ in range(n))
    m = rng.randint(1, n - 1)
    pattern = ''.join(rng.choice('ab') for _ in range(m))
    return [s, pattern]
